excerpt: Complete breakdown of Apex Trader Funding pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
Apex Trader Funding Pricing Overview

Apex Trader Funding, founded in 2021 and based in Austin, Texas, has positioned itself as an accessible entry point to proprietary futures trading. The platform offers a straightforward pricing model with monthly evaluation fees that scale based on the account size you want to trade. Starting at just $147 per month for a $25K evaluation account, Apex makes it possible for budget-conscious traders to begin their prop firm journey without massive upfront costs. The highest tier, a $300K evaluation, costs $517 per month—still relatively affordable compared to some competitors who charge percentage-based fees on funded accounts.

Unlike multi-phase evaluation systems where you pay once and fail upward through levels, Apex uses a one-step evaluation model. This means clearer expectations upfront: you know exactly what you're paying each month, and you reach your funded account directly if you meet the evaluation criteria. The pricing structure reflects Apex's focus on futures trading with paper trading tools, mobile access, and educational resources built into all tiers.

All Apex Trader Funding Plans Compared

Apex Trader Funding offers five evaluation tiers, each designed for traders with different account sizes and risk tolerances:

Evaluation Tier Monthly Cost Account Size Best For
Starter Evaluation $147/month $25,000 Prop firm newcomers, tight budgets
Growing Trader $167/month $50,000 Experienced day traders, moderate volume
Professional Trader $207/month $100,000 Active traders, consistent strategies
Elite Trader $297/month $150,000 High-volume traders, complex strategies
Institution $517/month $300,000 Serious traders, multi-contract positions

All tiers include the same core feature set: paper trading capabilities, mobile app access, performance analytics, risk management tools, social trading features, and educational resources. The key difference is the account size you can evaluate with and eventually trade. Every tier also comes with the same profit-sharing structure: you keep 100% of your first $25,000 in profits, regardless of which evaluation you're in. This is one of Apex's strongest selling points compared to competitors who may offer lower percentages or take splits earlier.

Free Plan / Free Trial

Apex Trader Funding does not offer a completely free tier or trial period. However, the platform provides paper trading functionality across all tiers, which allows you to test trading strategies and learn the platform without risking real capital. If you're evaluating whether Apex is right for you, this paper trading environment is where you can develop confidence before paying your first monthly fee.

The lowest-cost entry point remains the Starter Evaluation at $147/month for a $25K account. While this isn't free, it's one of the most affordable ways to access a prop firm evaluation. Consider this a small investment in testing whether futures trading and Apex's specific rules suit your style—especially given the one-step evaluation structure means you're not paying multiple times to advance through phases.

Hidden Costs and Fees

Apex's pricing structure is relatively transparent, but several costs can accumulate beyond the monthly evaluation fee:

  • Exchange and Regulatory Fees: While not explicitly charged by Apex, futures trading comes with exchange fees and NFA regulatory fees that may be deducted from your account or profits. These vary by contract and market.
  • Reset/Re-evaluation Fees: If you fail your evaluation or reset your account to attempt again, you'll pay the monthly fee again. Multiple resets add up quickly—some traders report paying $300–$500 across multiple attempts.
  • Payout Processing: Apex withdrawals take 5–10 business days to settle. While not a direct fee, this delay can impact traders who need rapid capital access.
  • Data Feed Costs: Real-time market data may require subscriptions depending on your broker integration and which futures contracts you trade.
  • Platform Integration Fees: If you use third-party trading tools or APIs with Apex, those services may have their own costs.

The key insight: Apex's monthly fees are fixed and clear, but cumulative costs from multiple reset attempts or broker fees can make the true cost of trading higher than the base subscription suggests.

Apex Trader Funding Pricing vs Competitors

Apex competes in a crowded prop firm space. While specific competitor details are best reviewed through TradingToolsHub's dedicated competitor comparison pages, here's what makes Apex's pricing position unique:

  • One-Step Evaluation: Most competitors use 2–4 phase evaluations with fees at each stage. Apex's single-phase model means fewer total costs if you pass on your first attempt.
  • 100% Profit Share on First $25K: Many competitors offer 70–80% of profits. Apex's full 100% on the initial $25K chunk is generous and attracts traders who prioritize profit retention over low evaluation costs.
  • No Daily Drawdown Limit: Some competitors impose daily loss caps that trigger automatic account halts. Apex's absence of this rule appeals to scalpers and high-frequency traders.
  • Discount Promotions: Apex frequently runs promotions offering 50–80% off evaluation fees. This can reduce your first month to as low as $30–$73, significantly undercutting competitors.
  • Futures-Only Focus: Unlike multi-asset competitors offering forex, stocks, and crypto, Apex is specialized. This limits scope but ensures tools and rules are optimized for futures.

For an detailed comparison with other prop firms' current pricing, see our prop firm comparison page and full Apex Trader Funding review.

Is Apex Trader Funding Worth the Price?

For Futures Traders on a Budget: At $147/month, the Starter Evaluation is one of the cheapest paths to a prop firm. If you're learning futures trading and want real-time trading without risking personal capital, this offers exceptional value. The one-step evaluation also reduces total cost of failure compared to multi-phase firms.

For Experienced Futures Traders: The $50K–$300K tiers ($167–$517/month) are worth it if you're consistently profitable and want access to larger accounts. The 100% profit share on the first $25K means even on a $50K account, you're earning back 2–3 months of fees within your first few profitable weeks. The no daily drawdown limit and ability to trade news events give edge-seeking traders freedom that competitors restrict.

For Scalpers and High-Volume Traders: The Elite and Institution tiers ($297–$517/month) pencil out quickly if you're doing 20+ trades daily. Your fee is recovered in 1–2 weeks of consistent profits, making Apex a solid ROI.

When to Upgrade: If you're repeatedly hitting the trailing threshold on your current tier, upgrade. If you're maxing out profits on a $25K account consistently, the $50K tier only adds $20/month but doubles your trading capital—a no-brainer economics. Similarly, moving from $100K to $150K tier adds just $90/month for 50% more capital.

Red Flag: If you're failing evaluations repeatedly and paying $147 monthly without progress, consider whether Apex's rules fit your strategy. Multiple resets mean $300+ spent on evaluations alone, not including exchange fees.

How to Save on Apex Trader Funding

Discount Promotions: Apex runs frequent promotional campaigns offering 50–80% off evaluation fees. Watch for these during market downturns or platform updates. A 70% discount could reduce the Starter Evaluation to $44/month or the $100K tier to $62/month—exceptional value. Subscribe to Apex's email list or check their social media for current codes.

Sign Up During Off-Peak Times: Promotions are more generous during slower trading periods (summer, holidays) or when Apex launches new features. Timing your registration around these windows can yield bigger discounts.

Annual Bundling: While Apex doesn't explicitly advertise annual pricing in their standard tiers, some regional promotions include 3–6 month prepay discounts. Contact their support to ask if bulk purchasing options exist for your tier.

Referral Programs: Some prop firms offer referral bonuses. If Apex has an active referral program, bringing in other traders can offset your own costs. Check their website for current terms.

Paper Trading First: Use the included paper trading environment to perfect your strategy before paying. This prevents costly failed evaluations and resets, indirectly saving money through fewer retry attempts.

Apex Trader Funding delivers accessible pricing for futures traders who value simplicity, profit retention, and flexible rules. At $147–$517/month depending on account size, it's competitively priced, especially with frequent promotions that can halve your costs. The real value emerges once you're profitable—the 100% profit share and no daily drawdown limits reward consistent traders faster than competitors.
